user_prompt:
User Requirement: do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 10m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-5.

Reference Directory Structure (similar case): <dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [U, epsilon, k, nuTilda, nut, p]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [momentumTransport, physicalProperties]</dir>

There are 6 files in Directory: 0,
There are 4 files in Directory: system,
There are 2 files in Directory: constant

Make sure you generate all the necessary files for the user's requirements.Do not include any gmsh files like .geo etc. in the subtasks.Only include blockMesh or snappyHexMesh if the user hasnt requested for gmsh mesh or user isnt using an external uploaded custom meshPlease generate the output as structured JSON.
response: subtasks=[SubtaskModel(file_name='U', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='p', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='k', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='epsilon', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='nut', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='nuTilda', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant'), SubtaskModel(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant')]
